title: The Iran War’s Strategic Fallout
content: By leaving US allies and rivals alike scrambling to respond to an unpredictable and unreliable superpower, the war in the Middle East is driving an historic geopolitical realignment that will shift the global balance of power over the next decade. The scale of the changes will rival those following the end of the Cold War.

creator: Ian Bremmer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran Standoff and the Future of Oil
content: The Iranians know that the usual US endgame in a war of choice is to walk away from defeat, take the humiliation, and move on. The most likely outcome in the current confrontation, then, is that Iran will prevail, the oil price will fall, and the US private-equity interests that have profited from the war will go bust.
snippet: The Iranians know that the usual US endgame in a war of choice is to walk away from defeat, take the humiliation, and move on. The most likely outcome in the current confrontation, then, is that Iran
creator: James K. Galbraith
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Should Europe Respond to the Energy Shock?
content: So far, European governments’ response to the Iran energy shock has missed the mark. Rather than subsidizing energy consumption by reducing fuel taxes, they should be pushing forward with a system that discourages emissions, incentivizes the adoption of renewables, and generates public revenues.
snippet: So far, European governments’ response to the Iran energy shock has missed the mark. Rather than subsidizing energy consumption by reducing fuel taxes, they should be pushing forward with a system tha
creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Putin Should Be Challenged to Restore Local Democracy in Russia
content: Ever since the American and French Revolutions, which took markedly different paths, democracy has grown most steadily when it has been rooted in local politics. Those who want to see a genuine democracy develop in Russia should begin there.
snippet: Ever since the American and French Revolutions, which took markedly different paths, democracy has grown most steadily when it has been rooted in local politics. Those who want to see a genuine democr
creator: Roger Myerson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Fiscal Discipline Requires More than Rules
content: While there is no easy fix for rising government debt levels, research and experience have shown that a well-designed fiscal framework can tilt policy choices toward financial discipline. Two areas, in particular, could be improved upon.
snippet: While there is no easy fix for rising government debt levels, research and experience have shown that a well-designed fiscal framework can tilt policy choices toward financial discipline. Two areas, i
creator: Antonio Fatas
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will AI Democratize Skills?
content: While AI does not eliminate the role of human capital in performance or equalize fundamental abilities, new research finds that it relaxes execution constraints that are more binding for less-educated individuals. But firms and governments must ensure that the workers who stand to benefit the most from these tools can access them.
snippet: While AI does not eliminate the role of human capital in performance or equalize fundamental abilities, new research finds that it relaxes execution constraints that are more binding for less-educated
creator: María Lombardi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Livable Cities Are the Key to India’s Growth
content: India is urbanizing rapidly, but poor public services, weak local governance, and unregulated sprawl are impeding productivity growth. The solution lies in reforms that empower local governments, improve administrative efficiency, and strengthen coordination of city planning and infrastructure development.
snippet: India is urbanizing rapidly, but poor public services, weak local governance, and unregulated sprawl are impeding productivity growth. The solution lies in reforms that empower local governments, impr
creator: Shishir Gupta
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Four Scenarios for the Iran War
content: After failing to clinch a decisive victory against Iran, US President Donald Trump is now facing deteriorating macroeconomic conditions ahead of November's midterm elections. With his options limited, he has been left to choose between wishful thinking and a high-risk re-escalation of hostilities.
snippet: After failing to clinch a decisive victory against Iran, US President Donald Trump is now facing deteriorating macroeconomic conditions ahead of November's midterm elections. With his options limited,
creator: Nouriel Roubini
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China’s Return to Infrastructure-Led Growth
content: Given China’s disappointing forays into consumption stimulus over the past few years, there can be little doubt that its recent strategic shift back toward infrastructure-driven growth is fully justified. All the talk among international commentators about overcapacity ignores the realities on the ground.
snippet: Given China’s disappointing forays into consumption stimulus over the past few years, there can be little doubt that its recent strategic shift back toward infrastructure-driven growth is fully justif
creator: Yu Yongding
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Jerome Powell’s Finest Hour
content: Just as Winston Churchill is primarily remembered for facing down the Nazi onslaught in World War II, outgoing US Federal Reserve Jerome Powell is likely to be remembered for his courageous defense of the Fed’s independence against President Donald Trump’s attacks. Like Churchill, he will be forgiven his major policy errors.
snippet: Just as Winston Churchill is primarily remembered for facing down the Nazi onslaught in World War II, outgoing US Federal Reserve Jerome Powell is likely to be remembered for his courageous defense of
creator: Desmond Lachman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Accounting for Atrocity
content: When it comes to prosecuting a toppled regime’s killers, torturers, and enablers, a balance must be struck between justice and stability. Syria’s new leaders are realizing, like Charles de Gaulle and Konrad Adenauer before them, that they need the support of tainted elites to rebuild their broken societies.
snippet: When it comes to prosecuting a toppled regime’s killers, torturers, and enablers, a balance must be struck between justice and stability. Syria’s new leaders are realizing, like Charles de Gaulle and
creator: Ian Buruma
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Hungarian Playbook for Defeating Populists?
content: Advocates of liberal democracy everywhere are celebrating Péter Magyar’s triumph over Viktor Orbán. But among the lessons to draw from the Hungarian experience, the most important may be that toppling an illiberal populist is not as straightforward as much of the press coverage has made it seem.
snippet: Advocates of liberal democracy everywhere are celebrating Péter Magyar’s triumph over Viktor Orbán. But among the lessons to draw from the Hungarian experience, the most important may be that toppling
creator: Jan-Werner Mueller
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Consensus on China’s Economy Is Strong—and Wrong
content: Calling on China to change its globally distortionary development strategy may well be warranted. But critics cannot question Chinese mercantilism on the grounds that it penalizes China's consumers, whose standard of living has risen faster than anywhere else in history.
snippet: Calling on China to change its globally distortionary development strategy may well be warranted. But critics cannot question Chinese mercantilism on the grounds that it penalizes China's consumers, w
creator: Arvind Subramanian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Geopolitical Battle Over Monetary Infrastructure
content: The United States has raised the alarm about Brazil’s instant payment platform, Pix. That is because the public scheme has highlighted the infrastructural basis of monetary sovereignty, which now hinges not on exchange rates and reserve currencies but on who designs and governs the rails on which finance moves.
snippet: The United States has raised the alarm about Brazil’s instant payment platform, Pix. That is because the public scheme has highlighted the infrastructural basis of monetary sovereignty, which now hing
creator: Camila Villard Duran
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The High Cost of Trump’s Crony Diplomacy
content: Effective diplomacy depends on credibility, consistency, and a clear alignment with national interests. The US administration’s personalized, opaque, and venal shadow diplomacy delivers none of that, and it will leave the US less respected, less trusted, and less effective on the world stage.
snippet: Effective diplomacy depends on credibility, consistency, and a clear alignment with national interests. The US administration’s personalized, opaque, and venal shadow diplomacy delivers none of that,
creator: Brahma Chellaney
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Has De-Dollarization Begun?
content: US President Donald Trump’s military adventures, attacks on long-standing allies, and dismantling of institutions like USAID are eroding the trust on which the dollar’s global primacy ultimately rests. The world’s reserve currency may have already begun its long, slow decline.
snippet: US President Donald Trump’s military adventures, attacks on long-standing allies, and dismantling of institutions like USAID are eroding the trust on which the dollar’s global primacy ultimately rests
creator: Kaushik Basu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A US-India Partnership for the AI Age
content: Although the United States maintains a decisive edge in frontier AI models and high-end compute, China has proven that its capacity for innovation is a force to be reckoned with. To prevent the Chinese government from claiming the strategic high ground, the US needs the talent and data that only India can provide.
snippet: Although the United States maintains a decisive edge in frontier AI models and high-end compute, China has proven that its capacity for innovation is a force to be reckoned with. To prevent the Chines
creator: Ylli Bajraktari
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: After Hungary, Could Mexico Be Next?
content: During his 16-year rule, Hungary's Viktor Orbán could plausibly argue that his model delivered order, stability, and rising living standards. But Mexico's government can make no such claim, suggesting that the country might find itself facing a destabilizing political transition sooner than expected.
snippet: During his 16-year rule, Hungary's Viktor Orbán could plausibly argue that his model delivered order, stability, and rising living standards. But Mexico's government can make no such claim, suggesting
creator: Guillermo Ortiz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Many Divisions Has Pope Leo?
content: Following the Trump administration's spat with Pope Leo XIV over its misbegotten war with Iran, one is reminded of previous historical episodes when the Vatican offered moral clarity for a world that had been corrupted by cynicism and aggressive authoritarianism. Americans, above all, should heed the message.
snippet: Following the Trump administration's spat with Pope Leo XIV over its misbegotten war with Iran, one is reminded of previous historical episodes when the Vatican offered moral clarity for a world that
creator: Harold James
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What China Will Learn from Orbán’s Defeat
content: After watching Nikita Khrushchev’s repudiation of Stalin lead to a revolution in Hungary, Mao Zedong concluded that under one-party rule, no amount of political liberalization is safe. The defeat of Viktor Orbán in Hungary’s parliamentary elections last month most likely reinforced that lesson for Chinese President Xi Jinping.
snippet: After watching Nikita Khrushchev’s repudiation of Stalin lead to a revolution in Hungary, Mao Zedong concluded that under one-party rule, no amount of political liberalization is safe. The defeat of V
creator: Sam Chetwin George
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Debtor Countries Finally Have a Group of Their Own
content: As systemic and geopolitical risks mount, financial uncertainty has become the new normal for developing countries, leading to a growing chorus of Global South policymakers calling for more collaboration and coordination. The newly formed Borrowers’ Platform makes this a reality.
snippet: As systemic and geopolitical risks mount, financial uncertainty has become the new normal for developing countries, leading to a growing chorus of Global South policymakers calling for more collaborat
creator: Ahmed Kouchouk
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Jerome Powell’s Mixed Legacy
content: By remaining on the US Federal Reserve board of governors after the end of his term as chair, Jerome Powell can keep the focus on his widely celebrated role as the “defender of the Fed.” But if that allows him to sideline substantive criticism of Fed policy during his tenure, the institution may suffer for it.

creator: Mohamed A. El-Erian
